fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

sectionMetadata.label: Plan of Care and Physical Therapy Evaluation
Footer (Not Visible in Print Preview)
sectionMetadata.notes: Fax server headers, signatures, and page chrome should stay out of the printable review surface.

textSpans.text: Patient is coming in for his preop exam.
/ feel the patient's right hip symptoms and previous imaging are severe enough to proceed with a right total hip
Risks and benefits of the proposed procedure were discussed in detail with the patient.
The patient did express understanding, all questions were answered.
Clearance has been received and reviewed today on patient.
Instructions: evaluate and treat per diagnosis/objective exam, post-operative protocol, and home health
Complications can include infection, blood clots, nerve artery vein tendon or bone injury, continued pain, the need
Alert and oriented to person, place and time.
Hip Osteoarthritis, Right (Patient Management Risk Level: Low)
Patient has increased VTE risk and will be placed back on his Eliquis following surgery for DVT and prior heart
Hip Osteoarthritis, Right (Patient Management Risk Level: Low) Patient has increased VTE risk and will be placed back on his Eliquis following surgery for DVT and prior heart

referralDashboardSections.label: Plan of Care and Physical Therapy Evaluation

textSpans.text: Indication: Hip Osteoarthritis, Right - right hip joint - M.16.11
Recommend frequency of 2-3 times per week for 6-8;weeks.
- Therapeutic Exercises: All exercises prn per therapist.
- Manual Therapy: All manual therapy prn per therapist.
- Modalities: All modalities prn per therapist.
HPI: This is a 78 year old male who:
1. is being seen for a preoperative evaluation.
He is scheduled for a right mako assisted anterior total hip
He is scheduled proceed with a right anterior total hip arthroplasty.
notes that he continues to have significant right hip pain that is affecting his activities of daily living.
tried and failed conservative treatment and wishes to proceed with surgery as scheduled.
Of note patient he is on
Eliquis and is to stop it 48 hours prior to surgery per his primary care.
Repair of meniscus: Rt & Lt knee
Other: Rt & Lt shoulder rotary cup
Repair of multiple tears of rotator cuff
for further surgery amongst other things.
discussed what type of implants will be used.
He is able to proceed with surgery as scheduled.
EtOH less than 1 drink per day:
Please refer to the education handout for detailed counseling.
Drives in the Daytime: Grocery doctor
Electronically Signed By: Karlene Gonzales, PA-C, 02/27/2026 12:10 PM MST
Electronically Signed By: Karlene Gonzales, PA-C, 02/16/2026 02:41 PM MST

consistencyChecks.title: M1700/M1710 vs Mental Status selections
consistencyChecks.detail: Referral documents describe mental-status evidence as Visit Note February 16, 2026 notes that he continues to have significant right hip pain that is affecting his activities of daily living. Of note patient he is on Impression/Plan: Plan: Counseling - Preoperative Instructions., and the chart snapshot does not currently show corresponding mental-status selections.

consistencyChecks.title: B1000 vision impairment vs glasses selection
consistencyChecks.detail: The chart snapshot does not currently show a vision-related entry, so B1000 vision impairment and any glasses selection still require reconciliation from chart evidence.

consistencyChecks.title: Respiratory findings vs M1400 shortness of breath answer
consistencyChecks.detail: Referral documents describe respiratory support with Visit Note February 16, 2026 notes that he continues to have significant right hip pain that is affecting his activities of daily living. Of note patient he is on Impression/Plan: Plan: Counseling - Preoperative Instructions.. Chart respiratory status currently reads blank.

consistencyChecks.title: Functional M items vs GG0130 and GG0170 scores
consistencyChecks.detail: Referral documents support function with weakness, gait, transfer, and endurance limitations. Chart GG0130/GG0170 values currently read self-care=blank and mobility=blank.

consistencyChecks.title: Wound answers vs integumentary details and wound worksheet
consistencyChecks.detail: Referral documents support wound status with DIAGNOSES Diagnosis Instructions: evaluate and treat per diagnosis/objective exam, post-operative protocol, and home health. Chart wound/Norton support currently reads integumentary=blank and Norton=blank.

consistencyChecks.title: Pain presence vs J0510/J0520/J0530 logic
consistencyChecks.detail: Referral documents show pain-related support as pain medication and pain-management instructions. Chart pain narrative currently reads Pain in the right hip with a scale of 5 out of 10; comfort measures and pain medication were provided. Re-checked after 30 minutes, resulting with a good relief..

consistencyChecks.title: Depression diagnosis/documentation vs D0150 completion
consistencyChecks.detail: Referral documents support mood history with DIAGNOSES Diagnosis Instructions: evaluate and treat per diagnosis/objective exam, post-operative protocol, and home health. Chart emotional or behavioral status currently reads blank.

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: Ralph E. Pierce, a 78-year-old male, is being admitted to home health care following a recent right total hip arthroplasty performed on February 26, 2026. This surgical intervention was necessitated by significant pain that impaired his activities of daily living, despite previous conservative treatments. Post-operatively, Mr. Pierce demonstrates functional limitations, including decreased mobility and difficulty ambulating without assistance, necessitating a structured rehabilitation program. Mr. Pierce requires s
chartSnapshot.pain_assessment_narrative: Pain in the right hip with a scale of 5 out of 10; comfort measures and pain medication were provided. Re-checked after 30 minutes, resulting with a good relief.
